issues: Headline is wrong: the Sockeyes did NOT sweep — they won in five games (not four), and the OCR uses the scoreline 'Packers 2 Sockeyes 3' as the header, not a sweep headline. The actual headline from the OCR appears to be absent as large bold text but the article makes clear it was a five-game series win, not a sweep.
Player name misspelled in draft: 'Jason Philips' is spelled 'Phillips' in the roster but appears as 'Philips' in the OCR — the draft should reflect the OCR spelling or correct it consistently.
The long description omits that the game was tied 2-2 going into the final period (Pat Mangold tied it at 1:37 of the third on a power play), making the win more dramatic and contextually important.
The long description omits Matt Hervey's shoulder separation injury suffered Friday in Kelowna, which is a notable detail in the article.
The long description omits the upcoming Mowat Cup context: Richmond would next face the PCJHL champion (Quesnel leading Grande Prairie 2-0) in the best-of-three provincial final starting April 3-5.
The draft headline 'Richmond Sockeyes Sweep Kelowna Packers' is factually inaccurate — the series went five games, not a sweep (which would be four games in a best-of-nine).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Kelowna Packers 3-2 to win the BCJHL championship series in five games, extending their playoff winning streak to 13.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes edged the Kelowna Packers 3-2 on March 24, 1987 at Richmond Arena, winning the best-of-nine BCJHL championship series in five games before more than 1,100 fans. Jason Philips scored the winner at 9:33 of the third period, flipping a rebound past Kelowna goaltender Dusty Imoo after Pat Mangold had tied the game 2-2 on a power play; goaltender Frank Romeo was credited for a strong performance despite battling sinusitis. The win stretched Richmond's playoff winning streak to 13 games and set up a Mowat Cup provincial final, tentatively scheduled for April 3-5 in Richmond, against the PCJHL champion. Veteran defenceman Matt Hervey, who suffered a shoulder separation in Kelowna on Friday, watched the third period from the parking lot while rehabilitating.
review.notes: The AI-generated headline "Richmond Sockeyes Sweep Kelowna Packers" is factually wrong — a five-game series in a best-of-nine is not a sweep. The OCR's in-article score header "Packers 2 Sockeyes 3" is used as the corrected headline since no large standalone headline is visible in the OCR. Editor should confirm the print headline if a cleaner scan is available. Also note the article spells the player's name "Philips" (one 'l') — roster has "Phillips" — OCR spelling retained in corrections.
